canonical_definition: Multilingual Over-Refusal Benchmark, a large-scale, automatically constructed evaluation set designed to assess overrefusal behaviors in LLMs across 7 languages and 12 safety categories by targeting prompts near the safety decision boundary.
papers: ‘Rich Dad, Poor Lad’: How do Large Language Models Contextualize Socioeconomic Factors in College Admission ?

definitions.text: Multilingual Over-Refusal Benchmark, a large-scale, automatically constructed evaluation set designed to assess overrefusal behaviors in LLMs across 7 languages and 12 safety categories by targeting prompts near the safety decision boundary.
definitions.paper: ‘Rich Dad, Poor Lad’: How do Large Language Models Contextualize Socioeconomic Factors in College Admission ?

definitions.text: A publicly released benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' tendencies to overrefuse across multiple languages, containing carefully constructed benign prompts that trigger refusal.

relationships.paper: ‘Rich Dad, Poor Lad’: How do Large Language Models Contextualize Socioeconomic Factors in College Admission ?
relationships.evidence: We have explored the safety decision boundaries of various LLMs and construct the MORBENCH evaluation set to facilitate robust assessment of model safety and helpfulness across multiple languages. (Abstract)
